channel.title: Comments for page "Note: A Part of Nobody"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Note: A Part of Nobody"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I am glad you liked it. As for the reincarnation aspect, that was done rather excellently in The Man Who Wasn't There Canon. I was attempting to take Nobody in a bit of a different direction and flesh out aspects that haven't really been touched on previously. That said, I certainly wouldn't be opposed to writing something that delves more into those aspects at some point in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was my feeling. Something like Nobody doesn't need extensive, detailed notes; just little bits here and there of "oh, is this what I am?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like its terseness. It really feels like notes. The font is just a little small, but I still read it fine. +1

I kind of wish that there is some tale done that explores the reincarnation aspect of Nobody, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I was worried about that. I couldn't really justify writing too much info, as it wouldn't really make sense for these notes. After all, if they went on too long, they wouldn't really help Nobody remember important information. I was hoping to pack in enough interesting stuff and hinted narrative that the short length wouldn't be an issue. If I didn't quite hit the mark, I suppose that's understandable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I removed the title on purpose. I felt it looked awkward, especially since the title is repeated in the text. However, seeing as how a blank title seems to mess with some Wikidot functions (like the discussion page), I'll add it back in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I found this much, much too short to have any real impact. What's there is interesting, sure, but this two-page format for Nobody is just tiny. Another few pages and this could have possibly gone to some interesting places; as-is, it just kind of feels insubstantial.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't read the font

EDIT: warning sign I might need glasses =(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You kind of forgot to add the title, bub.
